items.summary: Generator functions in agents/droid/generators/ mix template string construction, feature flag checking, and output formatting

evidence: Each generator function builds 200+ line Python source strings as triple-quoted templates while also checking feature flags, constructing imports, and managing indentation.
No separation between data-gathering, template-rendering, and validation phases.
Similar patterns repeated across 6+ generator files without a shared rendering abstraction.
detail.suggestion: Extract a template rendering layer. Each generator should call: gather_module_config() -> render_template(config) -> validate_output(code). The current single-function approach makes the generators untestable in isolation.

items.primary_command: desloppify issues

items.summary: 458 functions exceed 60 lines; generator functions in agents/droid remain 200-250 lines each

evidence: AST analysis found 458 functions/methods exceeding 60 lines after current fixes.
Top offenders remain: content_generators.py functions 200-243 lines each mixing template rendering, conditional checks, and output formatting.
documentation.py generator functions 230+ lines each.
run_command in subprocess.py 213 lines mixing process spawning, buffering, timeout, and error formatting.
detail.suggestion: Decompose the top 20 functions over 150 lines into focused helpers. Code generator functions should delegate to render_header(), render_body(), render_footer() helpers.

items.primary_command: desloppify issues

items.summary: 401 ABC/Protocol/abstractmethod uses across the codebase with many single-implementation abstract base classes

evidence: llm/providers/ correctly uses LLMProvider ABC with 4 concrete implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Gemini) — justified abstraction.
api/webhooks/transport.py WebhookTransport ABC has 2 implementations (HTTPWebhookTransport + abstract) — borderline justified.
Many single-implementation ABCs in coding/ and agents/ add indirection without actual polymorphism in use.
401 occurrences is 3x what would be expected for a codebase with genuine polymorphism needs.
detail.suggestion: Audit ABCs with exactly 1 concrete implementation and no documented extension plan. Replace with concrete classes directly. The llm/providers/ pattern is correct; smaller utility ABCs are over-engineered.

items.primary_command: desloppify issues

items.summary: MCP tool wrappers in many modules forward arguments to underlying implementations with minimal policy or validation added

evidence: 407 dynamic MCP tools registered — most are thin @mcp_tool decorated functions that call one underlying implementation function.
Pattern: @mcp_tool decorator registers function, function calls same-named implementation with identical args.
Few wrappers add validation, auth checks, rate limiting, or error translation beyond what the underlying function provides.
Some mcp_tools.py files have restating docstrings that duplicate the function signature in prose.
detail.suggestion: Audit each MCP tool to confirm it adds at least one of: input validation, auth check, error translation, or logging. Remove wrappers that add nothing and expose underlying functions directly via @mcp_tool.

items.primary_command: desloppify issues

items.summary: 126+ optional dependency groups in pyproject.toml lack documentation about which groups conflict with each other

evidence: pyproject.toml has 126+ [project.optional-dependencies] sections — one per module.
The aider module explicitly cannot coexist with in-project deps due to numpy/pyyaml pin conflicts.
No docs/DEPENDENCIES.md or compatibility matrix explaining which extras conflict.
Users doing 'uv sync --all-extras' may encounter silent conflicts.
detail.suggestion: Add a docs/DEPENDENCIES.md compatibility matrix. Document which extras conflict (e.g. aider must use 'uv tool install'). Add a CI check that validates the most common extras install cleanly together.

items.primary_command: desloppify issues

items.summary: Shell utilities return dicts while agent methods raise exceptions; no documented convention per module type

evidence: orchestrator/thin.py schema drift fixed — timeout and error returns now match success schema keys.
468 return-None patterns mixed with raise patterns across the codebase.
Shell execution utilities consistently return dicts with success/returncode/stdout/stderr — this pattern is now consistent within that module.
Agent call methods consistently raise exceptions — also consistent within that module.
The inconsistency is cross-module-type rather than within a module type.
detail.suggestion: Document the convention: shell utilities return dicts (for subprocess interop), agent methods raise typed exceptions (for Python caller ergonomics). Apply consistently within each module category.

items.primary_command: desloppify issues

items.summary: 128 top-level modules at codomyrmex root are navigable only by knowing module names — no domain-level wayfinding

evidence: 128 directories directly under src/codomyrmex/ — a new engineer must read the README or CLAUDE.md to understand which modules relate to which domain.
api, api_documentation, api_standardization, api_testing, api_tester appear as siblings without clear boundary documentation between them.
CLAUDE.md documents 4 layers but 128 modules do not map cleanly — many are cross-cutting.
detail.suggestion: Add a MODULES.md index that groups the 128 modules into 10 domain clusters with one-line descriptions. This costs nothing architecturally but dramatically improves orientation for new contributors.

items.primary_command: desloppify issues

items.summary: api, api_documentation, api_standardization, api_testing, api_tester are sibling modules sharing domain concern without documented boundaries

evidence: 5 modules share 'api' in their name and likely share concern without clear stated boundaries between them.
No shared parent directory grouping them — they are siblings of logging_monitoring, orchestrator, etc.
Similar pattern: agents, agents/core, agents/history, agentic_memory live at different hierarchy levels.
detail.suggestion: Document in each module's README.md where that module ends and adjacent modules begin. The flat structure can work if each module clearly states its specific responsibility boundary.

items.primary_command: desloppify issues

items.summary: Desloppify test health metric includes 1600+ standalone scripts as untested modules, distorting the production code coverage picture

evidence: 1629 open test_coverage findings before wontfix cleanup — vast majority were scripts/ and projects/ example code.
After marking scripts/* and projects/* as wontfix, production src/codomyrmex/ modules are the remaining coverage gap.
Trust gateway has 25 tests, MCP bridge has 49 tests — core high-risk paths have reasonable coverage.
agents/history module tests confirmed to exist at tests/unit/agents/test_history_*.py.
Production modules still untested: agentic_memory/obsidian/models.py, agents/evaluation/__init__.py, api/pagination/__init__.py, cache/warmers/__init__.py.
detail.suggestion: Add tests for the 10 highest-LOC untested production modules. Prioritize modules with multiple importers (high blast radius). Skip standalone scripts — they legitimately have no test harness.

narrative.headline: ⚠ 125 security findings — review before other cleanup. Score plateaued at 73.1 for 5 scans. Test health (21.3%) is where the breakthrough is. 54 wontfix items may also be worth revisiting. (9 review findings — run `desloppify issues`)

actions.description: 9 review findings need investigation — run `desloppify issues` to see the work queue
11 flat_dirs findings — create subdirectories and use `desloppify move`
29 coupling findings — fix boundary violations with `desloppify move`
251 facade findings — flatten re-export facades or consolidate barrel files
5 uncalled_functions findings — remove dead functions or document why they're retained
761 structural findings — decompose large files — extract logic into focused modules
6 props findings — split bloated components, extract sub-components
181 dupes findings — extract shared utility or consolidate duplicates
288 dict_keys findings — fix dict key mismatches — dead writes are likely dead code, schema drift suggests a typo or missed rename
1668 test_coverage findings — add tests for untested production modules — prioritize by import count
75 responsibility_cohesion findings — split modules with too many responsibilities — extract focused sub-modules
326 boilerplate_duplication findings — extract shared boilerplate into reusable helpers or base classes
118 unused findings — inspect with `desloppify next` and fix manually
1585 smells findings — inspect with `desloppify next` and fix manually
42 global_mutable_config findings — refactor module-level mutable state — use explicit init functions or dependency injection
9 stale_wontfix findings — re-evaluate old wontfix decisions — fix, document, or escalate
125 security findings — review and fix security findings — prioritize by severity

strategy.hint: 4 independent workstreams, safe to parallelize. Rescan after each phase to verify. Review: 9 finding(s) — `desloppify issues`.

primary_action.description: 9 review findings need investigation — run `desloppify issues` to see the work queue

narrative.why_now: 4 independent workstreams, safe to parallelize. Rescan after each phase to verify. Review: 9 finding(s) — `desloppify issues`.

risk_flags.message: Strict/lenient gap is 0.2 pts with 54 wontfix findings
